chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D – 3966 of 2025 a/w Const. Petition Nos. D – 3967, D-3968, D-4004, D-4005, D-4086 and D-4124 of 2025 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1. For orders on office objection 2. For orders on CMA No.16263/2025 3. For hearing of main case 15.04.2026 Mr. Abdul Ahad, Advocate for the petitioners in all the petitions. M/s. Muhammad Saad Siddiqui and Sahibzada Muhammad Mubeen, Advocates for respondent No.1 in C.P. Nos. D-4004 and D- 4087 of 2025. Mr. Faizan Hussain Memon, Advocate for respondents in C.P. No. D-4086, D-4087 and D-4124 of 2025. Ms. Shahzia Hanjrah, DAG. ------------------------------------ Learned counsel for respondent No.1 in C.P. Nos. D-4004 and D-4087 of 2025 places on record copy of order dated 17.02.2026 penned down by a learned Single Bench, Islamabad High Court in FAO No.66 of 2018 (Re- Pakistan Telecom Mobile Limited vs. Pakistan Telecommunication Authority) and submits that these petitions be disposed of in view of the directions contained under the said order. A copy of the order is handed over by counsel for respondent No.1 to the counsel for petitioner, who upon receipt of the same seeks disposal of these petitions Before parting with the order, it will be beneficial to reproduce he contents of the said order, which reads as under: “ The learned AAG in attendance has informed this Court that the Member (Judicial) as well as the Member (Technical) of the Telecommunication Appellate Tribunal have been appointed, whereas the post of Member (Finance) is still vacant, for which the process has been initiated.

Pakistan Telecommunication Authority) and submits that these petitions be disposed of in view of the directions contained under the said order. A copy of the order is handed over by counsel for respondent No.1 to the counsel for petitioner, who upon receipt of the same seeks disposal of these petitions Before parting with the order, it will be beneficial to reproduce he contents of the said order, which reads as under: “ The learned AAG in attendance has informed this Court that the Member (Judicial) as well as the Member (Technical) of the Telecommunication Appellate Tribunal have been appointed, whereas the post of Member (Finance) is still vacant, for which the process has been initiated. He further contends that he will contact the relevant authorities with regard to the appointment to the vacant post of the Tribunal and will apprise this Court in this regard on the next date of hearing. 2. At this stage, the learned counsel for PTA has submitted a list of cases pending before Islamabad High Court, which are 43 in number, which includes FAOs, CMAs, and Writ Petitions out of which certain cases are required to be transferred to the Telecommunication Appellate Tribunal established under 2 Establishment of Telecommunication Appellate Tribunal Act, 2024 in terms of Section 7-A of the Pakistan Telecommunication (Re- organization) Act inserted vide Section 4 of the Act ibid. Copy of the list has been placed on record. 3. In view of the above, Registrar of this Court is directed to transmit the cases which falls under the purview of the Tribunal, except the case in hand to the Telecommunication Appellate Tribunal before the next date of hearing for further proceedings. 4. Instant case be listed on 10.03.2026, for the 4. report qua appointment of Member (Finance) as well as for the report that cases have been transferred to the Tribunal and the Tribunal has become functional, where-after, this case will be transferred, accordingly. In view of the above all these petitions are hereby disposed of and the Registrar of this Court is directed to transmit these cases in terms of Section 7-A of the Pakistan Telecommunication (Re-organization) Act inserted vide Section 4 of the Act, which falls under the purview of the Telecommunication Appellate Tribunal by retaining a photocopy of mem of petition for record.

Instant case be listed on 10.03.2026, for the 4. report qua appointment of Member (Finance) as well as for the report that cases have been transferred to the Tribunal and the Tribunal has become functional, where-after, this case will be transferred, accordingly. In view of the above all these petitions are hereby disposed of and the Registrar of this Court is directed to transmit these cases in terms of Section 7-A of the Pakistan Telecommunication (Re-organization) Act inserted vide Section 4 of the Act, which falls under the purview of the Telecommunication Appellate Tribunal by retaining a photocopy of mem of petition for record. However, in the meantime, unless the petitioners may approach the appropriate forum, the interim order passed earlier to continue. Office to place a copy of this order in the connected petitions. JUDGE HEAD OF CONST. BENHCES JUDGE Azeem
